prompt:
Ticket: team-wiki bookmarks service. Plain TypeScript on node:http — this runs inside our sidecar, zero npm packages, and it sits behind a keep-alive-heavy internal proxy, which is why half this ticket is about connection discipline. Deliver at least two modules (the acceptance tests import exactly these): store.ts exporting class BookmarkStore plus error types ValidationError (with a .fields record) and DuplicateUrlError (with .url), and app.ts exporting createApp(options?) that returns an http.Server WITHOUT listening — the tests call listen(0) themselves. Split routing/body-reading helpers into more files if you like; I'd rather review three small files than one wall.

createApp options: { store?, maxBodyBytes? } — cap defaults to 65536.

The resource. A bookmark is { id, url, title, tags }. Ids are 'bm-1', 'bm-2', ... strictly increasing, never reused, even after deletes. Validation (report EVERY bad field at once in fields, and reject unknown fields by name): url required, trimmed, must parse as an absolute http/https URL; title required, trimmed, non-blank; tags optional array of non-blank strings, normalized trimmed+lowercased, deduped keeping first appearance. Duplicate policy: two bookmarks may not share the exact same trimmed url — that's a 409 over HTTP and a DuplicateUrlError from the store (updating a bookmark to its own url is fine). Store lookups return copies; handing a caller a reference into the store is how we got burned last time. update() checks existence BEFORE validating (missing id → undefined, no throw). list({ tag? }) filters on the normalized tag.

Routes and statuses: GET /bookmarks → 200 {"items":[...],"count":n} in creation order, optional ?tag= filter (normalized). POST /bookmarks → 201 + Location: /bookmarks/<id> + the record. GET/PATCH/DELETE /bookmarks/<id> → 200 / 200 / empty 204, 404 when absent. Strict paths: /bookmarks/ and /bookmarks/<id>/extra are 404s, unknown paths 404. Method not routable on a known path → 405 with an Allow header listing exactly what is ('GET, POST' or 'GET, PATCH, DELETE'). Every body-carrying response is application/json with a Content-Length (no chunked responses): {"error":"not_found"|"method_not_allowed"|"invalid_json"|"invalid_body"|"validation" (+fields)|"duplicate_url" (+url)|"payload_too_large" (+limit)|"unsupported_media_type"}.

Bodies, and get this exactly right. POST/PATCH require a Content-Type whose media type is application/json (parameters like charset are fine; anything else, or none, is a 415). Body parse: not JSON → 400 invalid_json; JSON but not an object → 400 invalid_body. The size cap is where connections go to die, so: a request DECLARING Content-Length over the cap is answered 413 immediately — do not read the body first — with Connection: close, and the server closes that socket after the response. A chunked/streamed body that crosses the cap mid-read gets the same 413 + Connection: close + close. But an error found AFTER the body was fully read within the cap (bad JSON, validation, 409, 415-with-small-body — drain it) must NOT cost the connection: respond and keep the socket usable for the next request. test_wire.ts drives raw sockets to check exactly this.

Acceptance: node --test test_store.ts test_api.ts test_wire.ts — all three files are the contract, don't touch them. Loopback only, 127.0.0.1.
